missions.title: Motion in a Straight Line
missions.description: Master the fundamentals of 1D motion, including displacement, velocity, acceleration, and kinematic equations.

microtargets.title: Distance vs Displacement
ncertAnchors: NCERT Physics Class 11, Chapter 3: Motion in a Straight Line - 3.2 Position, Path Length and Displacement
mustKnowFacts: Distance is a scalar quantity representing total path length.
Displacement is a vector quantity representing the shortest distance between initial and final positions.
Magnitude of displacement is always less than or equal to distance.
commonTraps: Assuming displacement and distance are the same for a returning journey (displacement is zero, distance is 2d).
Forgetting to assign negative signs for displacement in the negative x-direction.
masteryCriteria: Can calculate distance and displacement for a particle reversing its direction.
pyqPatterns: Ratio of distance to displacement for a particle moving in a semicircle.

activeRecallQuestions.question: What is the ratio of distance to displacement for a particle completing half a circle of radius R?

microtargets.title: Average vs Instantaneous Velocity
ncertAnchors: NCERT Physics Class 11, Chapter 3: 3.3 Average Velocity and Average Speed, 3.4 Instantaneous Velocity and Speed
mustKnowFacts: Average velocity is total displacement divided by total time.
Average speed is total distance divided by total time.
Instantaneous velocity is the derivative of position with respect to time (dx/dt).
For equal distances traveled at speeds v1 and v2, average speed is 2*v1*v2/(v1+v2).
commonTraps: Using arithmetic mean (v1+v2)/2 for average speed when distances are equal instead of harmonic mean.
Confusing average velocity (vector) with average speed (scalar).
masteryCriteria: Can derive average speed for multi-part journeys with different time or distance intervals.
pyqPatterns: Particle covers first half distance at v1 and second half at v2.

activeRecallQuestions.question: A car covers the first half of a distance at 40 km/h and the second half at 60 km/h. What is its average speed?

microtargets.title: Velocity-Time Graph Area
ncertAnchors: NCERT Physics Class 11, Chapter 3: Kinematics Graphs
mustKnowFacts: The area under a Velocity-Time (v-t) graph represents Displacement.
Area above the time axis is positive displacement, area below is negative displacement.
The total area (sum of absolute values) represents total Distance traveled.

formulas.name: Displacement from v-t Graph
formulas.expression: Δx = ∫ v dt = Area under v-t curve

commonTraps: Subtracting negative area when calculating total distance.
Adding negative area when calculating displacement (it must be subtracted).
masteryCriteria: Can reliably distinguish between distance and displacement calculated from a v-t graph spanning positive and negative velocities.
pyqPatterns: Finding distance vs displacement for a triangular v-t graph that dips below the x-axis.

activeRecallQuestions.question: What does the area under a velocity-time graph represent, and how does it differ for calculating distance vs displacement?
expectedAnswerPoints: For distance, take absolute value of all areas
For displacement, subtract areas below the time axis

microtargets.title: Equations of Uniformly Accelerated Motion
ncertAnchors: NCERT Physics Class 11, Chapter 3: 3.6 Kinematic Equations for Uniformly Accelerated Motion
mustKnowFacts: Equations of motion are ONLY valid when acceleration is constant.
v = u + at
s = ut + 1/2 at^2
Displacement in nth second: Sn = u + a/2 * (2n - 1)
commonTraps: Using v=u+at when acceleration is variable (e.g., a function of time).
Confusing S_n (displacement in nth second) with S (total displacement in n seconds).
masteryCriteria: Can correctly select the appropriate equation based on known and unknown variables.
pyqPatterns: Stopping distance of a car (s = u^2 / 2a).
Ratio of distances covered in 1st, 2nd, 3rd seconds (Galileo's law of odd numbers).

activeRecallQuestions.question: If the initial speed of a car is doubled, how does its minimum stopping distance change (assuming constant braking deceleration)?
expectedAnswerPoints: Stopping distance is proportional to u squared

microtargets.title: Motion Under Gravity (Free Fall)
ncertAnchors: NCERT Physics Class 11, Chapter 3: Motion under Gravity
mustKnowFacts: Acceleration is strictly downward, a = -g (approx -9.8 m/s^2).
Time of ascent equals time of descent (in absence of air resistance).
Velocity with which an object is thrown up is equal to velocity with which it returns to the projection point.
commonTraps: Using positive g when the object is moving upwards (if upward is taken as positive).
Forgetting that displacement is zero when a ball is thrown up and caught at the same level.
masteryCriteria: Can solve cases where an object is dropped from a rising balloon or moving helicopter.
pyqPatterns: Ball dropped from a balloon moving upwards at constant velocity.
Water drops falling at regular time intervals.

activeRecallQuestions.question: A stone is dropped from a balloon rising upwards with velocity u. What is the initial velocity of the stone relative to the ground?
expectedAnswerPoints: The stone inherits the balloon's velocity

missions.title: Motion in a Plane (2D)
missions.description: Understand vectors, projectile motion, and relative velocity in two dimensions.

microtargets.title: Projectile Motion: Time of Flight and Range
ncertAnchors: NCERT Physics Class 11, Chapter 4: Motion in a Plane - 4.10 Projectile Motion
mustKnowFacts: Horizontal motion is uniform velocity (ax = 0).
Vertical motion is uniformly accelerated (ay = -g).
Horizontal range is maximum when angle of projection is 45 degrees.
For complementary angles (θ and 90-θ), the horizontal range is the same.
commonTraps: Applying 1D kinematic equations without resolving into horizontal and vertical components.
Assuming velocity is zero at the highest point (only vertical velocity is zero, horizontal is u*cosθ).
masteryCriteria: Can calculate range, height, and time of flight, and use the trajectory equation for complex obstacles.
pyqPatterns: Relation between Range and Maximum Height (R*tanθ = 4H).
Kinetic energy at the highest point.

activeRecallQuestions.question: What is the speed and acceleration of a projectile at its maximum height?

microtargets.title: Relative Velocity in 2D (Rain-Man, River-Boat)
ncertAnchors: NCERT Physics Class 11, Chapter 4: 4.11 Relative Velocity in Two Dimensions
mustKnowFacts: V_AB = V_A - V_B (Velocity of A with respect to B).
In river-boat problems, to cross in minimum time, boat should be rowed perpendicular to river flow.
To cross along the shortest path (straight across), boat must be rowed at an angle upstream.
In rain-man problems, angle of umbrella is determined by the direction of relative velocity of rain with respect to man.
commonTraps: Adding vectors algebraically instead of geometrically.
Confusing 'velocity of boat in still water' with 'actual velocity of boat relative to ground'.
masteryCriteria: Can confidently draw vector triangles to solve rain-man and river-boat problems.
pyqPatterns: Finding the angle to hold the umbrella when a man is running.
Finding drift of a boat crossing a river.

activeRecallQuestions.question: In what direction should a swimmer swim to cross a river in the shortest possible time?
